findings.title: WiFi Packet Captures from Starbucks Public Network
findings.description: User captured network traffic at Starbucks (starbucks pcap.pcap, starbucks.csv in Documents). Combined with NetStumbler and Acrylic WiFi Professional installation and confirmed execution via Prefetch, this constitutes unauthorized network interception of third-party communications.
findings.recommendation: Analyze starbucks pcap.pcap for captured credentials, session cookies, or PII. Chain of custody preservation required. Potential violation of CFAA and wiretap statutes.

findings.title: Documents Related to Weaponizing Technology
findings.description: User Documents folder contains files with highly sensitive titles: 'Research to Weaponize the Ion Thruster.docx', 'ZF DNA splice test notes.docx', 'Rapid cell regeneration research.docx'. The ion thruster component specification (ic-enhanced-spec-sheet.pdf) was also recently accessed per LNK analysis.
evidence: /mnt/windows/Users/PC User/Documents/Research to Weaponize the Ion Thruster.docx
findings.recommendation: Extract and review document contents immediately. Escalate to relevant law enforcement and intelligence agencies for classification and national security assessment.

findings.title: SDelete Deployed to System32 and Executed — Evidence Destruction
findings.description: SDelete (Sysinternals secure deletion utility) was placed in C:\Windows\System32\ — an unusual location for a portable tool — and executed, confirmed by Prefetch. This indicates deliberate anti-forensics activity to destroy evidence prior to device seizure.
findings.recommendation: Perform VSS (Volume Shadow Copy) analysis to recover deleted files. Carve unallocated space with PhotoRec/Scalpel. Timeline correlation to identify which files were deleted.

findings.title: Random-Named PE32 Executable in Temp Directory
findings.description: set_PxRcHIFy.exe found in AppData\Local\Temp. Randomized gibberish filenames in Temp are a hallmark pattern for malware droppers, RATs, and stagers. PE32 executable with 5 sections.
findings.recommendation: Submit MD5 to VirusTotal and MalShare. Perform static analysis (PE header, imports, sections). Detonate in sandbox. Cross-reference with Prefetch for execution time.

findings.title: NETWIORK LICENSE SERVER — Typosquatted Executable with HTTP/FTP Exfiltration Capability
findings.description: NETWIORK LICENSE SERVER 3.4.1.exe uses an intentional typo ('NETWIORK' vs 'NETWORK') consistent with social engineering and detection evasion. String analysis reveals WinINet HTTP functions (HttpQueryInfoW, HttpOpenRequestW, HttpSendRequestW) and FTP (FtpFindFirstFileA), inconsistent with a legitimate license server. Requires administrator privileges per embedded manifest.
evidence: Strings: HttpQueryInfoW, HttpOpenRequestW, HttpSendRequestW, HttpSendRequestExW, HttpEndRequestW
findings.recommendation: Submit to VirusTotal. Examine network logs for outbound HTTP/FTP connections. Determine C2 infrastructure via dynamic analysis. Certificate may identify actor.

findings.title: VeraCrypt Installed — Encrypted Volume Created (FORMAT Executed)
findings.description: VeraCrypt was downloaded, installed, and the FORMAT tool executed — confirming at least one encrypted volume was created. No .vc containers found on C:, suggesting they reside on external media or were deleted (see F003 — SDelete used after VeraCrypt FORMAT).
findings.recommendation: Seize and image any external USB drives associated with the device. Check OneDrive sync logs for uploaded container files. Analyze VSS snapshots before SDelete wiped them.

findings.title: Security Event Log on Desktop — Counter-Forensic Self-Monitoring
findings.description: security.evtx (26,737 events) found on Desktop — an unusual location for an event log. This indicates the subject copied and reviewed their own security log, likely to identify whether their activity was being monitored or to clean specific entries.
evidence: Type: MS Windows Vista-8.1 Event Log, 313 chunks, 26738 records
Evtx analysis confirms real identity: anthony.vanko@gmail.com (Event 4648/4624)
findings.recommendation: Compare Desktop copy against System32/winevt/Logs/Security.evtx — record count and hash differences indicate log tampering.

findings.title: NinaResearch — Targeted Intelligence Gathering on Individual
findings.description: Documents folder contains NinaResearch/ directory and NinaResearch.zip. Person-specific research collections warrant investigation for stalking, corporate espionage, or intelligence operations.
findings.recommendation: Extract and examine NinaResearch.zip contents. Determine subject's identity and relationship to device owner.

timeline_highlights: 2016-04-26 13:41: First logon with anthony.vanko@gmail.com (Event 4648/4624, Logon Type 7)
2016: Acrylic WiFi Professional installed and run
2016: VeraCrypt FORMAT executed — encrypted volume created
2016-11-04 13:47-14:32: Physical image acquired by Ovie Carroll (DFIR.training)
false_positive_notes: CNN.EXE confirmed as legitimate Windows Store app (CNN App for Windows, path: WindowsApps/588E6FFA.CNNApp...). DismHost.exe instances in Temp are legitimate Windows DISM deployment components. Google Update and OneDrive executables in AppData are legitimate signed binaries. DABACLUPDATE.EXE prefetch strings were non-readable (compressed) — could not confirm malicious without further analysis.

files: Documents/Research to Weaponize the Ion Thruster.docx
tools_of_evil: NETWIORK LICENSE SERVER — suspected RAT/data exfiltration
